context: Tests whether the agent applies no_log to sensitive tasks, keeps secrets out of plaintext group_vars, uses quoted octal permissions, and avoids shell/command for user management. Maps to the secrets management, no_log, and file permissions instructions.

checklist.name: no_log on password tasks
checklist.description: Every task that references a password variable (pg_readonly_password, pg_writer_password, pg_admin_password) has `no_log: true`

checklist.name: No plaintext secrets in vars.yml
checklist.description: The `group_vars/postgres_servers/vars.yml` file does NOT contain any password or secret values — only non-sensitive configuration

checklist.name: Vault file uses vault references
checklist.description: The `group_vars/postgres_servers/vault.yml` file uses `!vault |` prefixes or otherwise indicates vault-encrypted values (not plaintext passwords)

checklist.name: Quoted octal file permissions
checklist.description: The pg_hba.conf deployment task uses a quoted octal mode string (e.g., `'0640'`)

checklist.name: FQCN modules used
checklist.description: All module references use FQCN (e.g., `ansible.builtin.template`, `ansible.builtin.user` or community module FQCN)

checklist.name: All tasks have name field
checklist.description: Every task in the playbook has a `name:` field

checklist.name: Boolean true/false syntax
checklist.description: All boolean values use `true` or `false`, not `yes` or `no`

checklist.name: No shell/command for user creation
checklist.description: Database user creation does NOT use `ansible.builtin.shell` or `ansible.builtin.command` when a dedicated module (ansible.builtin.user, community.postgresql.*) is appropriate
